text.title: Product-Led Growth (PM Angle) Core Rules
text.summary: Metric-driven PLG loop (visitor -> signup -> activated -> PQL -> SQL -> paying -> expansion) with PM-owned aha-moment instrumentation, activation rituals, and expansion-revenue accountability.

rule.statement: The aha moment MUST be defined as a single event (or short event sequence) reachable in <=10 min from signup, with explicit instrumentation tracking time-to-aha per cohort.
rule.rationale: Without aha instrumentation, activation work optimises proxies (DAU).

rule.statement: Product Qualified Lead criteria MUST be written down: {events triggered, frequency, account-shape filters}. PQL != MQL.
rule.rationale: Implicit PQL definitions drift across the team.

rule.statement: Free->paid conversion MUST be tracked by activation cohort, not by signup cohort.
rule.rationale: Signup-cohort conversion is structurally lower and misleading.

rule.statement: PM owns expansion revenue (seat growth, usage upgrades, plan upsell), not just acquisition.
rule.rationale: Expansion handed to sales loses the product lever.

rule.statement: Activation experiments MUST be throttled to <=2 concurrent in the same funnel step.
rule.rationale: Stacked experiments confound; <=2 concurrent keeps causal attribution clean.

rule.statement: If applicability conditions fail (any Skip-If trigger holds — enterprise-sales-only; B2B with no self-serve; aha structurally >10min; commodity competition), the agent MUST skip this methodology and route to the named alternative or to the methodology fallback.
rule.rationale: Mis-applying a methodology outside its preconditions degrades output quality and pollutes downstream artefacts; the explicit skip-rule keeps the routing tree honest.
